# build-doctor

> Automatic test failure fixer for claude-code-factory

## What it does
When a factory build fails `make test`, build-doctor reads the error log from SQLite, clones the repo, sends errors + source code to Claude Code CLI, applies fixes, and retries until tests pass. After 3 failed attempts, marks the build as permanently failed.

## Commands
- `build-doctor fix` — fix all failed builds
- `build-doctor fix --name <repo>` — fix one repo
- `build-doctor stats` — show fix success rates
- `build-doctor serve` — HTTP monitoring API

## Integration
Part of the claude-code-factory autonomous software pipeline. Reads from the shared `build_queue` SQLite table and writes status updates back.

## Source
- Repository: https://github.com/timholm/build-doctor
- Language: Go
- License: MIT
